description: Set the value of ball attributes. This command is a synonym for the ball initialize command. Ball attributes are characteristics of the balls such as position or size. These are distinct from ball properties (assigned with the ball property command) that are surface properties of the balls that are used to fill contact model properties.

modifiers.name: gradient
modifiers.description: Apply linear gradient in each axis direction starting at origin

modifiers.name: gradient-x/y/z
modifiers.description: Apply linear gradient in specific direction

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: Use Ball.set_*() methods (e.g., Ball.set_density(), Ball.set_vel(), Ball.set_pos()) - requires iteration over ball range

keywords.name: damp
keywords.description: Ball local damping coefficient where f ≥ 0. By default, f = 0.

keywords.name: density
keywords.description: Ball density, where f > 0. By default, ball density is set to zero upon creation of a ball; a non-zero value is required for PFC to properly compute mass properties and integrate the equation of motion for the ball. An exception is thrown if a ball with zero density exists in the model when cycling starts.

keywords.name: displacement
keywords.description: Accumulated ball displacement vector as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: displacement-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the accumulated ball displacement as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: displacement-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the accumulated ball displacement as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: displacement-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the accumulated ball displacement as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: euler
keywords.description: Current orientation of Euler angles following the X,Y,Z convention (e.g., rotation about the \(x\) -axis followed by rotation about the y’ -axis followed by rotation about the z’‘ -axis) in degrees. The orientation is updated only when orientation tracking has been enabled (see model orientation-tracking command). When active, the current ball orientation can be visualized.

keywords.name: euler-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -euler angle (in degrees) of the current ball orientation. See the euler keyword for further details.

keywords.name: euler-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -euler angle (in degrees) of the current ball orientation. See the euler keyword for further details.

keywords.name: euler-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -euler angle (in degrees) of the current ball orientation. See the euler keyword for further details.

keywords.name: force-applied
keywords.description: Force applied to balls.

keywords.name: force-applied-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the force applied to balls.

keywords.name: force-applied-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the force applied to balls.

keywords.name: force-applied-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the force applied to balls.

keywords.name: force-contact
keywords.description: Sum of the contact force accumulated to the balls during the previous force-displacement update. This value will be modified during the next force-displacement update.

keywords.name: force-contact-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the contact force.

keywords.name: force-contact-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the contact force.

keywords.name: force-contact-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the contact force.

keywords.name: fragment
keywords.description: Fragment ID (see “ Fragment ”).

keywords.name: moment-applied
keywords.description: Moment applied to balls.

keywords.name: moment-applied-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the moment applied to balls.

keywords.name: moment-applied-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the moment applied to balls.

keywords.name: moment-applied-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the moment applied to balls.

keywords.name: moment-contact
keywords.description: Sum of the contact moments accumulated to the balls during the previous force-displacement update. This value will be modified during the next force-displacement update.

keywords.name: moment-contact-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the contact moment.

keywords.name: moment-contact-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the contact moment.

keywords.name: moment-contact-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the contact moment.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: Location of ball centroid. Note that the model should be cleaned (see the model clean command) after this command is issued to ensure that potential contacts with neighboring pieces are updated.

keywords.name: position-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the location of ball centroids.

keywords.name: position-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the location of ball centroids.

keywords.name: position-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the location of ball centroids.

keywords.name: radius
keywords.description: Ball radius where f > 0. A positive radius is required at ball creation (using the ball create , ball generate , or ball distribute commands). Ball radii can be subsequently modified using this command. Note that the model should be cleaned (see the model clean command) after this command is issued to ensure that potential contacts with neighboring pieces are updated.

keywords.name: rotation
keywords.description: Current ball orientation. The orientation is updated only when orientation tracking has been enabled (see model orientation-tracking command).

keywords.name: spin
keywords.description: Ball angular velocity.

keywords.name: spin-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the ball angular velocity in radians.

keywords.name: spin-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the ball angular velocity in radians.

keywords.name: spin-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the ball angular velocity in radians.

keywords.name: velocity
keywords.description: Ball translational velocity vector.

keywords.name: velocity-x
keywords.description: The \(x\) -component of the ball velocity.

keywords.name: velocity-y
keywords.description: The \(y\) -component of the ball velocity.

keywords.name: velocity-z
keywords.description: The \(z\) -component of the ball velocity.

keywords.name: kwd:add
keywords.description: Add f to the existing value.

keywords.name: kwd:gradient
keywords.description: Apply a linear gradient in each of the axes directions starting at the origin.

keywords.name: kwd:gradient-x
keywords.description: Apply a linear gradient in the \(x\) -direction starting at the origin.

keywords.name: kwd:gradient-y
keywords.description: Apply a linear gradient in the \(y\) -direction starting at the origin.

keywords.name: kwd:gradient-z
keywords.description: Apply a linear gradient in the \(z\) -direction starting at the origin.

keywords.name: kwd:multiply
keywords.description: Multiply the existing value by f .

keywords.name: kwd:replace
keywords.description: Replace the existing value with f .

examples.description: Set density to 1000 and damping coefficient to 0.7 for all balls (most common usage)
Set initial velocity for balls in specified position range
Apply constant force to balls in group (not updated during cycling)

examples.command: ball attribute radius 0.5 multiply 1.2 range id 1 100
examples.description: Multiply radius by 1.2 for balls with ID 1-100 (use model clean after geometry changes)
